title: Special Loci for Local Systems
speaker: Bruno Klingler
abstract: Given a local system on a complex algebraic variety, what are the subvarieties on which the monodromy drops? The talk will discuss these monodromy special loci, a natural generalisation of (the positive period dimension components of) the Hodge loci.
tags: Workshop on Special Cycles and Related Topics

proof_stages.label: Motivation and monodromy-special definitions
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates replacing hard questions about fundamental groups of algebraic varieties by linear monodromy questions, fixes a complex local system, defines its connected algebraic monodromy group, and introduces small-monodromy, monodromy-special, and big monodromy loci for positive-dimensional subvarieties.

proof_stages.label: Hodge-theoretic model via period maps
proof_stages.summary: For integral variations of Hodge structure, period maps and semisimplicity decompose the big monodromy locus into fiber-family parts and isolated monodromy-special parts, identify the latter with suitable Hodge-locus/atypical-intersection phenomena, and recall finiteness and high-level strictness results.

proof_stages.label: Attempts beyond VHS: rank one and nonabelian Hodge theory
proof_stages.summary: The speaker explores how the rank-one case and the projective nonabelian Hodge correspondence suggest countable algebraic families of small-monodromy subvarieties, but also explains that this approach is nonexplicit and difficult in the quasi-projective case.

proof_stages.label: Betti-side theorem for reductive local systems
proof_stages.summary: Returning to the Betti side, the main theorem is stated: for a local system with positive-dimensional reductive algebraic monodromy, the big monodromy locus is a countable union of algebraic families, while the non-normal monodromy locus is a countable union of strongly rigid special subvarieties with corresponding field-of-definition consequences.

proof_stages.label: Kollár weakly complete families and proof of rigidity
proof_stages.summary: Using Kollár's existence of weakly complete topologically locally trivial families of normalizations of subvarieties, the proof shows that monodromy drop propagates along each family and that, in the non-normal monodromy locus, maximal components cannot move and hence have pointlike deformation space.

proof_stages.label: Generic variation of the local system and atypicality analogue
proof_stages.summary: By letting the representation vary in an irreducible component of the Zariski-dense representation variety, the monodromy locus is shown to be generically constant and upper-semicontinuous, yielding a strictness corollary when the component contains a high-level integral VHS and motivating a conjecture excluding only low-level CVHS cases.

proof_stages.label: Intrinsic rigid special locus and open problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk ends by proposing the union of all strongly rigid subvarieties of X as the intrinsic 'true special locus,' noting that it contains all the monodromy-special loci under consideration but is largely mysterious even in basic moduli spaces.
